frameworks.name: information theory
frameworks.one_line: Quantify perception by treating sensory events as an information-handling process.

frameworks.name: redundancy reduction
frameworks.one_line: Higher organisms face highly redundant sensory input across space and time.

core_question: How can the concepts and techniques of information theory clarify our understanding of visual perception?
key_assumptions: Perception can be conceptualized as an information-handling process
Sensory events are interdependent in both space and time
Events ordered in time are amenable to informational analysis
key_findings: Much of the information received by higher organisms is redundant
States of a limited set of receptors permit better-than-chance inferences about prior and subsequent states of those and other receptors
interpretations: Information theory provides new and more precise ways to conceptualize perceptual situations
Spatial and temporal interdependence of sensory events is a defining property to be exploited
